fs.notes: The shared sync fs bridge is cataloged here to document overlap with future WinterTC-style profiles, but no WinterTC fs surface is claimed yet.

fetch.notes: WHATWG Fetch JS object model is available through jsval_headers_*, jsval_request_*, jsval_response_*, and jsval_fetch. Headers supports case-insensitive append/set/get/has/delete, combine-with-comma reads, whitespace normalization, token validation, and getSetCookie. Request accepts URL-string or Request input plus an init dict (method/headers/body) with forbidden-method (CONNECT/TRACE/TRACK) rejection. Response supports body, status, statusText, headers, plus static Response.error, Response.redirect (301/302/303/307/308 only), and Response.json. The Body mixin (text/json/arrayBuffer/bytes) resolves synchronously against in-memory bytes. fetch() now delegates outbound through a pluggable jsval_fetch_transport_t vtable registered via jsval_region_set_fetch_transport — embedders hook begin/poll/cancel and the JSVAL_MICROTASK_KIND_FETCH_REQUEST microtask pumps the poll. Coroutine-driven embedders that can't run their I/O macros inside a poll callback use the fetch waitlist mode instead (jsval_region_set_fetch_waitlist_mode + jsval_fetch_waitlist_pop + jsval_fetch_waitlist_size) — jsval_fetch parks (Request, Promise) pairs in a region-local FIFO and the embedder's coroutine body drives them externally. mnvkd's vk_test_faas_demo uses the waitlist + vk_jsmx_fetch_driver sub-coroutine to run a transpiled proxy handler end to end against an upstream. Without a transport or waitlist mode set, jsval_fetch still rejects with DOMException{name:'TypeError', message:'network not implemented yet'} for backward compatibility. ReadableStream bodies, Blob, FormData, AbortSignal, TLS upstream, and redirect chains are still deferred.

crypto.notes: The current WinterTC crypto profile covers randomUUID, getRandomValues, stable subtle object identity, Promise-backed subtle.digest over the SHA digest family, Promise-backed HMAC generateKey/importKey/exportKey/sign/verify over raw and jwk key formats, Promise-backed AES-GCM generateKey/importKey/exportKey/encrypt/decrypt over raw and jwk key formats, Promise-backed AES-CTR generateKey/importKey/exportKey/encrypt/decrypt over raw and jwk key formats, Promise-backed AES-CBC generateKey/importKey/exportKey/encrypt/decrypt over raw and jwk key formats with PKCS#7 padding, Promise-backed AES-KW generateKey/importKey/exportKey over raw and jwk key formats, Promise-backed subtle.wrapKey/subtle.unwrapKey dispatching on either AES-KW or AES-GCM as the wrap cipher, over the raw and jwk formats, wrapping HMAC and AES-GCM/CTR/CBC/KW inner keys, Promise-backed PBKDF2 raw import plus deriveBits/deriveKey to HMAC, AES-GCM, AES-CTR, and AES-CBC, Promise-backed HKDF raw import plus deriveBits/deriveKey to HMAC, AES-GCM, AES-CTR, and AES-CBC, Promise-backed ECDSA P-256 generateKey/importKey/exportKey/sign/verify over raw (SEC1 uncompressed public only) and jwk key formats with SHA-256/384/512 hashes — sign/verify emit and consume WebCrypto-spec IEEE P1363 fixed-width r||s signatures, generateKey resolves to a CryptoKeyPair — and Promise-backed RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 generateKey/importKey/exportKey/sign/verify over jwk only, at 2048/3072/4096-bit modulus lengths with public exponent 65537, SHA-256/384/512 hashes, generateKey returning a CryptoKeyPair, and Promise-backed RSA-PSS generateKey/importKey/exportKey/sign/verify over jwk only (same modulus/exponent/hash policy) with per-operation saltLength defaulting to the hash output length when absent — randomized signatures suitable for PS256/PS384/PS512 JWT verification against FIPS 186-5 / PSD2 / PCI DSS v4 compliant IdPs. The asymmetric algorithms (ECDSA P-256, RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5, RSA-PSS, Ed25519) additionally support `spki` (public) and `pkcs8` (private) key transport formats for importKey/exportKey, unblocking RFC 7523 private-key JWT client authentication flows for Google Cloud service accounts, GitHub Apps, Apple App Store Connect, Salesforce, and similar SaaS APIs that ship DER-encoded key material. Ed25519 additionally supports raw (32-byte public) and jwk (RFC 8037 OKP kty) formats, with deterministic 64-byte signatures per RFC 8032 PureEdDSA — EdDSA JWT verification is now expressible against Keycloak, Authentik, GitHub Actions OIDC tokens, Matrix homeserver federation, and Apple device-attested tokens. Other subtle algorithms remain pending.
